followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Authorization class ko non-mockable banana — concrete class without interface, injected directly. Test mein real AUTHORITY-CHECK call hoti hai — tests depend on user's actual role assignments in the system. This makes tests environment-dependent and non-deterministic.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test mock: always authorized
CLASS lcl_mock_auth_allowed DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES zif_authorization.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lcl_mock_auth_allowed IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD zif_authorization~is_authorized.
    rv_auth = abap_true.  " Always authorized in this mock
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
" Test: inject authorized mock
METHOD test_authorized_user_gets_price.
  DATA(lo_auth) = NEW lcl_mock_auth_allowed( ).
  DATA(lo_sut)  = NEW zcl_price_service( io_auth = lo_auth ).
  DATA(lv_price) = lo_sut->get_price( iv_matnr = 'MAT001' ).
  cl_abap_unit_assert=>assert_not_initial( lv_price ).
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What authorization issue can occur in Factory pattern implementation?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Factory authorization issue: factory ne ek restricted pricing strategy object create kiya — lekin factory se pehle authorization check nahi hua. Koi bhi user factory call kar sakta hai — restricted strategy object mil jaata hai. Factory ko authorization-gate banana: factory mein pehle is calling user authorized hai ki nahi check karo — agar nahi toh ZCX_NOT_AUTHORIZED raise karo. Ek step aur: factory ko different strategies return karni chahiye based on user authorization level — limited user ko simpler strategy, authorized user ko full strategy.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Factory authorization issue: the factory creates objects that provide access to sensitive operations without checking if the calling user is authorized to use those objects. Fix: inject an authorization checker into the factory and validate before creating the restricted object. If the user is not authorized, raise ZCX_NOT_AUTHORIZED. More sophisticated: the factory returns different implementations based on user authorization level — an authorized user gets the full strategy, a restricted user gets a read-only or limited-capability strategy implementing the same interface. This leverages polymorphism for authorization-aware behavior.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Authorization-based factory is a real pattern in enterprise SAP: different users get different behavior from the same business process based on their role. Example: regular user gets read-only pricing object, manager gets editable pricing object — both implement zif_pricing_strategy, factory decides which based on authorization.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Authorization check only at UI layer (selection screen, Fiori app) without checking in backend class methods. A developer with SE38 access can call the class directly bypassing UI — backend checks are mandatory.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
CLASS zcl_strategy_factory DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS constructor
      IMPORTING io_auth TYPE REF TO zif_authorization.
    METHODS create_pricing_strategy
      IMPORTING iv_bukrs        TYPE bukrs
      RETURNING VALUE(ro_strat) TYPE REF TO zif_pricing_strategy
      RAISING   zcx_not_authorized.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA mo_auth TYPE REF TO zif_authorization.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_strategy_factory IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create_pricing_strategy.
    IF mo_auth->is_authorized(
         iv_object = 'Z_PRICING'
         iv_actvt  = '01' ) = abap_false.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_not_authorized.
    ENDIF.
    " Now safe to create strategy
    ro_strat = NEW zcl_full_pricing_strategy( ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you log authorization failures in OOABAP for audit purposes?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Authorization failure logging: ek security audit log rakhna compliance ke liye zaroori hai — koun, kab, kis object pe, kya fail hua. SLG1 (Application Log) use karo — external log object 'ZSECURITY', sub-object 'AUTH_FAIL'. Log entries: user, timestamp, authorization object, activity, program/class, business object (like vbeln). Agar sensitive operations hain — regulatory requirement ho sakta hai ki sab failed access attempts log ho. Security team ko regularly review karna chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Authorization failure logging for audit compliance: I use the ABAP Application Log (SLG1) with a dedicated object for security events. On each authorization failure, I log: user ID, timestamp, authorization object checked, activity attempted, the ABAP class and method where the check failed, and the business object being accessed (order number, material number, etc.). This creates an immutable audit trail. For regulatory environments (GDPR, SOX), this logging is mandatory. The log entries are written using CL_BALI_LOG and stored to DB via CL_BALI_LOG_DB to survive session termination.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Application log vs database table for security logging: SLG1 has built-in archiving, access control, and standard display. Custom Z-table gives more flexibility but needs manual archiving. SLG1 preferred for standard compliance — SAP audit tools already know how to read it.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Authorization failure sirf RAISE EXCEPTION karna bina logging ke — ek suspicious access pattern (one user failing 100 times) invisible rehta hai. Log karo, then raise. Security monitoring depends on this data.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
METHOD raise_authorization_error.
  " Log before raising
  DATA(lo_log) = cl_bali_log=>create(
    iv_object    = 'ZSECURITY'
    iv_subobject = 'AUTH_FAIL' ).
  lo_log->add_message(
    cl_bali_message_setter=>create(
      iv_severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error
      iv_text     = |Auth fail: { sy-uname } { iv_object } { iv_actvt }| ) ).
  cl_bali_log_db=>save_log_2nd_db_connection(
    io_log = lo_log ).
  " Now raise exception
  RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_not_authorized
    EXPORTING iv_object = iv_object
              iv_actvt  = iv_actvt.
ENDMETHOD.

followupAnswerBank.question: What authorization check should be done in Repository classes?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Repository class authorization: repository directly database se read karta hai — agar authorization check nahi hai toh koi bhi class jo repository use kare woh data access kar sakta hai. Repository mein: read operations pe AUTHORITY-CHECK with display activity (ACTVT '03'), write operations pe change activity (ACTVT '02'). Ek dedicated authorization object per business domain: V_VBAK_AAT (sales documents), M_MATE_MAR (materials). Repository should be the last line of defense — UI may have checks, but repository's check ensures backend security regardless of how it is called.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Repository classes are the data access layer and must enforce authorization as the final defense — they are called from multiple places including unit tests, RFC, Fiori backend, and direct ABAP calls. Each repository method should check the appropriate authorization object: display methods check ACTVT 03, change methods check ACTVT 02 or 01, delete methods check ACTVT 06. The authorization object maps to the business domain: V_VBAK_AAT for sales orders, M_MATE_MAR for materials, F_BKPF_BUK for FI documents. If the check fails, repository raises ZCX_NOT_AUTHORIZED — caller handles the exception and shows appropriate user message.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Repository authorization + testability: inject authorization checker via constructor (ZIF_AUTHORIZATION interface). Unit tests inject mock that returns abap_true — so repository logic can be tested independently of real authorizations. Integration tests run with real authorization setup in test client.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Repository mein authorization check nahi karna ye sochke ki 'caller already checked.' Callers change, new callers are added, direct ABAP access is possible — repository must be self-sufficient in its authorization enforcement.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
CLASS zcl_sales_order_repo IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_order.
    " Always check in repository - final defense
    IF mo_auth->is_authorized(
         iv_object = 'V_VBAK_AAT'
         iv_actvt  = '03' ) = abap_false.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_not_authorized
        EXPORTING iv_object = 'V_VBAK_AAT'
                  iv_actvt  = '03'.
    ENDIF.
    SELECT SINGLE vbeln, netwr, kunag
      FROM vbak WHERE vbeln = @iv_vbeln
      INTO @rs_order.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_order_not_found
        EXPORTING iv_vbeln = iv_vbeln.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does authorization change in S/4HANA OOABAP context?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: S/4HANA mein authorization ke naye dimensions: Fiori launchpad pe tile-level authorization (SAP_UI5_USER_AUTH), OData service level authorization (ZIF_* SADL callbacks), RAP behavior authorization (IS_AUTHORIZED method in behavior implementation class). AUTHORITY-CHECK still works but now additional layers hain. RAP mein: behavior definition mein authorization master tag karo, implementation mein is_authorized method override karo — object-level aur field-level authorization possible hai. Fiori mein user has correct role (S/4 roles) — ye Basis/Security team ka kaam hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In S/4HANA, authorization has additional layers beyond classic AUTHORITY-CHECK. RAP behavior implementation has a dedicated is_authorized method where you implement object-level and field-level authorization checks — this integrates with SAP's authorization framework for transactional Fiori apps. OData service-level authorization through SADL hooks provides another layer. Classic AUTHORITY-CHECK in ABAP class methods remains valid and is still the core mechanism. New additions: Business Role Management in S/4HANA for role assignment, and SAP Access Control integration for automated access governance. Developer responsibility remains: code-level AUTHORITY-CHECK in backend classes.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: RAP is_authorized method: implement in behavior handler class, override for each entity's operations. Return failed-<entity> for unauthorized access — RAP framework handles the 401 response to the Fiori app. This is the S/4HANA way of OO authorization in backend.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: S/4HANA Fiori app mein sirf frontend-level authorization karna (tile restriction) — backend ABAP classes must have their own checks. Frontend authorization prevents UI access, but direct OData calls or ABAP class calls still need backend checks.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" RAP behavior: is_authorized method
CLASS lhc_salesorder DEFINITION
  INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS get_instance_authorizations
      FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations
      RESULT result.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS lhc_salesorder IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).
      " Check: can this user change this order?
      AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'V_VBAK_AAT'
        ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '02'.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky         = ls_key-%tky
                        %update      = if_abap_behv=>auth-unauthorized )
               TO result.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.
